sourceMovie.title: Curse of Chucky
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Curse of Chucky works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Halloween
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Curse of Chucky clicked for you, Halloween (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Severance
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Curse of Chucky clicked for you, Severance (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Curse of Chucky?
faq.answer: Try Halloween, Severance, Annabelle: Creation—each shares DNA with Curse of Chucky while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Curse of Chucky?
faq.answer: Curse of Chucky (2013) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Curse of Chucky part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Child's Play Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
